content:
/-
Copyright (c) 2024 Archon. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Archon
-/
import MR2223407ConstructionHilbertQuot.Functors
import MR2223407ConstructionHilbertQuot.Regularity
import MR2223407ConstructionHilbertQuot.SemicontinuityBaseChange
import MR2223407ConstructionHilbertQuot.FlatteningStratification

/-!
# MR2223407: Construction of the Quot Scheme (Nitsure В§5)

This file scaffolds the blueprint chapter `chapters/QuotConstruction.tex` вҖ” the
representability of the projective Quot functor, in the AltmanвҖ“Kleiman form
(`thm:altman-kleiman-quot`, the project goal theorem) and the Grothendieck form
(`thm:grothendieck-quot`), together with the lemma chain assembling the proof.

Each declaration corresponds to a `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the blueprint:

* `ProjectiveMorphism`      вҖ” `def:projective-morphism`
* `StronglyProjective`      вҖ” `def:strongly-projective`
* `quot_grothendieck`       вҖ” `thm:grothendieck-quot`
* `quot_altmanKleiman`      вҖ” `thm:altman-kleiman-quot`  (project goal)
* `quot_projectiveSpace`    вҖ” `thm:quot-pn`
* `quot_reduction`          вҖ” `lem:quot-reduction`
* `quot_uniformRegularity`  вҖ” `lem:quot-uniform-regularity`
* `quot_embedGrassmannian`  вҖ” `lem:quot-embed-grassmannian`
* `quot_flatteningStratum`  вҖ” `lem:quot-flattening-stratum`
* `quot_properness`         вҖ” `lem:quot-properness`

## Conventions and stating-gaps

Representability of a moduli functor `Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{E/X/S}` "by a scheme `Q` over `S`"
is rendered, following the functor-of-points convention already used in
`Functors.grassmannian_represents`, as the existence of an object `Q : Over S`
together with a natural isomorphism between the moduli functor (valued in
`Type (u+1)`) and the (universe-lifted) functor of points `pointsFunctor Q`.  This
is the genuinely-expressible content of "represents"; naturality in the test scheme
is carried by the natural isomorphism.

The Serre twists `E(ОҪ)`, `F(r)`, the symmetric/exterior powers `Sym^r V`,
`вӢҖ^{ОҰ(r)}(W вҠ— Sym^r V)`, the relative Grassmannian of a bundle, and coherent
cohomology are all Mathlib gaps (recorded in `Basic.lean`, `Functors.lean`,
`Regularity.lean`, `SemicontinuityBaseChange.lean`, `FlatteningStratification.lean`).
Where a faithful statement would name one of these we either take it as abstract
input data (the established sibling-file convention вҖ” e.g. `twist : в„Ө вҶ’ _.Modules`)
or pass to the closest honest geometric shadow (`IsLocallyFreeOfRank` for "vector
bundle", `relativeProj S F` for `в„ҷ(F)`, `IsImmersion`/`IsClosedImmersion` for
locally-closed/closed embedding).  Per-declaration gaps are documented in the
doc-strings and in the task result.  No `axiom` is introduced.
-/

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits

universe u

namespace MR2223407ConstructionHilbertQuot

/-! ## Functor-of-points helper -/

/-- The functor of points of an `S`-scheme `A : Over S`, universe-lifted to land in
`Type (u+1)` so it can be compared with the moduli functors of this development
(`quotFunctorPhi`, valued in `Type (u+1)`).  `A` *represents* a moduli functor `M`
when `M вү… pointsFunctor A`.  This is a helper, not a blueprint block. -/
noncomputable def pointsFunctor {S : Scheme.{u}} (A : Over S) :
    (Over S)бө’бө– вҘӨ Type (u + 1) :=
  yoneda.obj A вӢҷ CategoryTheory.uliftFunctor.{u + 1}

/-! ## Notions of projectivity -/

/-- **Projective morphism (Grothendieck)** (`def:projective-morphism`).

A morphism `f : X вҹ¶ S` is *projective* if there is a coherent (here:
quasi-coherent) sheaf `E` on `S` together with a closed immersion
`i : X вҶӘ в„ҷ(E) = relativeProj S E` over `S`.

STATING-GAP: "coherent" вҶҰ "quasi-coherent" and `в„ҷ(E)` is the stub `relativeProj`
(relative `Proj` of a graded sheaf is a Mathlib gap, see `Basic.relativeProj`).
The equivalent "proper + relatively very ample line bundle" formulation is
`RelativelyVeryAmple` together with properness; the two are matched in the
blueprint. -/
def ProjectiveMorphism {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X вҹ¶ S) : Prop :=
  вҲғ (E : S.Modules), E.IsQuasicoherent вҲ§
    вҲғ (i : X вҹ¶ relativeProj S E),
      IsClosedImmersion i вҲ§ i вү« relativeProj.structureMorphism S E = f

/-- **Strongly projective morphism (AltmanвҖ“Kleiman)** (`def:strongly-projective`).

A morphism `f : X вҹ¶ S` is *strongly projective* if there is a vector bundle `E`
on `S` (locally free of some rank `d`) together with a closed immersion
`i : X вҶӘ в„ҷ(E)` over `S`.  This is the special case of `ProjectiveMorphism` in which
the sheaf `E` is required to be locally free.

STATING-GAP: "vector bundle" вҶҰ `IsLocallyFreeOfRank d` (a Mathlib-gap predicate
stated abstractly in `Functors.lean`); `в„ҷ(E)` is the stub `relativeProj`.  The
strongly *quasi*-projective variant (locally closed embedding) is not separately
named here. -/
def StronglyProjective {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X вҹ¶ S) : Prop :=
  вҲғ (E : S.Modules) (d : в„•), IsLocallyFreeOfRank d E вҲ§
    вҲғ (i : X вҹ¶ relativeProj S E),
      IsClosedImmersion i вҲ§ i вү« relativeProj.structureMorphism S E = f

/-! ## Main existence theorems -/

/-- **Grothendieck Quot scheme** (`thm:grothendieck-quot`).

For `S` noetherian, `ПҖ : X вҹ¶ S` projective with relatively very ample `L`, any
coherent `E` on `X` and any `ОҰ вҲҲ в„ҡ[О»]`, the functor `Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
representable by a *projective* `S`-scheme.

STATING-GAP: representability is the functor-of-points shadow `вү… pointsFunctor Q`
(see `pointsFunctor`); "projective `S`-scheme" is `ProjectiveMorphism Q.hom`.  The
proof (deferred) reduces to `quot_altmanKleiman` via the noetherian `m`-regularity
of `mumford_regularity` and `flatteningStratification` over an affine cover. -/
theorem quot_grothendieck {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (ПҖ : X вҹ¶ S)
    (_hПҖ : ProjectiveMorphism ПҖ) (L : X.Modules) (_hL : RelativelyVeryAmple ПҖ L)
    (E : X.Modules) (_hE : E.IsQuasicoherent) (ОҰ : Polynomial в„ҡ) :
    вҲғ Q : Over S, ProjectiveMorphism Q.hom вҲ§
      Nonempty (quotFunctorPhi ПҖ E L ОҰ вү… pointsFunctor Q) :=
  sorry

/-- **AltmanвҖ“Kleiman Quot scheme** (`thm:altman-kleiman-quot`) вҖ” *the project goal*.

Let `S` be noetherian, `X` a closed subscheme of `в„ҷ(V)` for a vector bundle `V` on
`S`, `L = O_{в„ҷ(V)}(1)|_X`, `E` a coherent quotient of `ПҖ^*(W)(ОҪ)` with `W` a vector
bundle on `S` and `ОҪ вҲҲ в„Ө`, and `ОҰ вҲҲ в„ҡ[О»]`.  Then `Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
representable by a scheme `Q` that embeds over `S` as a closed subscheme of `в„ҷ(F)`
for some vector bundle `F` on `S` вҖ” i.e. `Q вҹ¶ S` is *strongly projective*.

STATING-GAP: representability is the functor-of-points shadow `вү… pointsFunctor Q`;
"embeds as a closed subscheme of `в„ҷ(F)`, `F` a vector bundle" is exactly
`StronglyProjective Q.hom`.  The specific bundle `F = вӢҖ^{ОҰ(r)}(W вҠ— Sym^r V)` (whose
`вӢҖ`, `вҠ—`, `Sym^r` are Mathlib gaps) is captured up to its existence inside
`StronglyProjective`.  `V`, `W` vector bundles вҶҰ `IsLocallyFreeOfRank`; `в„ҷ(V)` is
the stub `relativeProj`; `L = O(1)|_X` вҶҰ `RelativelyVeryAmple ПҖ L`; the twist
`ПҖ^*(W)(ОҪ)` is abstract input `EWОҪ` surjecting onto `E`.  The proof is the
assembly of `quot_reduction`, `quot_uniformRegularity`, `quot_embedGrassmannian`,
`quot_flatteningStratum`, `quot_properness`. -/
theorem quot_altmanKleiman {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
    (V : S.Modules) (dV : в„•) (_hV : IsLocallyFreeOfRank dV V)
    (jX : X вҹ¶ relativeProj S V) (_hjX : IsClosedImmersion jX)
    (ПҖ : X вҹ¶ S) (_hПҖ : ПҖ = jX вү« relativeProj.structureMorphism S V)
    (L : X.Modules) (_hL : RelativelyVeryAmple ПҖ L)
    (W : S.Modules) (dW : в„•) (_hW : IsLocallyFreeOfRank dW W) (ОҪ : в„Ө)
    (EWОҪ E : X.Modules) (_hE : E.IsQuasicoherent) (qE : EWОҪ вҹ¶ E) (_hqE : Epi qE)
    (ОҰ : Polynomial в„ҡ) :
    вҲғ Q : Over S, StronglyProjective Q.hom вҲ§
      Nonempty (quotFunctorPhi ПҖ E L ОҰ вү… pointsFunctor Q) :=
  sorry

/-- **Quot scheme in projective space** (`thm:quot-pn`).

For `S` noetherian, `X` a closed subscheme of `в„ҷвҒҝ_S`, `L = O_{в„ҷвҒҝ_S}(1)|_X`, `E` a
coherent quotient of `вҠ•^p O_X(ОҪ)`, and `ОҰ вҲҲ в„ҡ[О»]`, the functor `Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{E/X/S}`
is representable by a scheme embedding over `S` as a closed subscheme of `в„ҷ^r_S`.

STATING-GAP: representability is `вү… pointsFunctor Q`; `в„ҷвҒҝ_S`, `в„ҷ^r_S` are
`projectiveSpace`; the twist `вҠ•^p O_X(ОҪ)` is abstract input `EOpОҪ` surjecting onto
`E`.  This is the trivial-bundle case `V = O_S^{n+1}`, `W = O_S^p` of
`quot_altmanKleiman`. -/
theorem quot_projectiveSpace {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (n : в„•)
    (jX : X вҹ¶ projectiveSpace n S) (_hjX : IsClosedImmersion jX)
    (ПҖ : X вҹ¶ S) (_hПҖ : ПҖ = jX вү« projectiveSpace.structureMorphism n S)
    (L : X.Modules) (_hL : RelativelyVeryAmple ПҖ L)
    (p : в„•) (ОҪ : в„Ө) (EOpОҪ E : X.Modules) (_hE : E.IsQuasicoherent)
    (qE : EOpОҪ вҹ¶ E) (_hqE : Epi qE) (ОҰ : Polynomial в„ҡ) :
    вҲғ (Q : Over S) (r : в„•) (i : Q.left вҹ¶ projectiveSpace r S),
      IsClosedImmersion i вҲ§
      i вү« projectiveSpace.structureMorphism r S = Q.hom вҲ§
      Nonempty (quotFunctorPhi ПҖ E L ОҰ вү… pointsFunctor Q) :=
  sorry

/-! ## Reduction to the model case -/

/-- **Reduction to the model case** (`lem:quot-reduction`).

(i) For any `ОҪ вҲҲ в„Ө`, tensoring by `L^ОҪ` gives an isomorphism of functors
`Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{E/X/S} вү… Quot^{ОЁ,L}_{E(ОҪ)/X/S}` with `ОЁ(О») = ОҰ(О» + ОҪ)`.
(ii) For any surjection `ПҶ : E вҶ’ G` of coherent sheaves on `X`, the induced natural
transformation `Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{G/X/S} вҶ’ Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{E/X/S}` is a closed embedding.

STATING-GAP: in (i) the twist `E(ОҪ)` is a Serre-twist gap; we phrase the claim as
"there exists a sheaf `EОҪ` (the twist `E(ОҪ)`) making the functors isomorphic", with
`ОЁ = ОҰ.comp(X + C ОҪ)`.  In (ii) "closed embedding of functors" (relative
representability by closed immersions) is shadowed by "the natural transformation is
a monomorphism".  Both are honest, non-vacuous shadows. -/
theorem quot_reduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} (ПҖ : X вҹ¶ S) (E L : X.Modules)
    (ОҰ : Polynomial в„ҡ) :
    (вҲҖ ОҪ : в„Ө, вҲғ EОҪ : X.Modules,
        Nonempty (quotFunctorPhi ПҖ E L ОҰ вү…
          quotFunctorPhi ПҖ EОҪ L (ОҰ.comp (Polynomial.X + Polynomial.C (ОҪ : в„ҡ))))) вҲ§
      (вҲҖ (G : X.Modules) (ПҶ : E вҹ¶ G), Epi ПҶ вҶ’
        вҲғ О· : quotFunctorPhi ПҖ G L ОҰ вҹ¶ quotFunctorPhi ПҖ E L ОҰ, Mono О·) :=
  sorry

/-! ## Use of `m`-regularity -/

/-- **Uniform `m`-regularity of the family** (`lem:quot-uniform-regularity`).

Working in the model case on `в„ҷвҒҝ` (with `n = rank V - 1`, `p = rank W`),
`mumford_regularity` supplies a single integer `m`, depending only on `rank V`,
`rank W` and `ОҰ`, so that for every member of every family the source, kernel and
quotient become `m`-regular; after twisting by `r вүҘ m` and pushing forward along
`ПҖ_T`, the direct images are locally free of fixed rank with vanishing higher direct
images.

STATING-GAP: the Serre twists `E_T(r)`, `F(r)`, `G(r)` are Mathlib gaps, so вҖ” as in
`Basic.serre_vanishing` and the sibling files вҖ” the twist families are abstract
input data `twistE, twistF, twistG : в„Ө вҶ’ (в„ҷвҒҝ_T).Modules`.  "Locally free of fixed
rank" is `IsLocallyFreeOfRank` of the underived pushforward
`Scheme.Modules.pushforward (ПҖ_T)`.  The fibrewise `m`-regularity bound, the precise
ranks (`rank ПҖ_{T*}F(r) = ОҰ(r)`, `ПҖ_*E(r) = W вҠ— Sym^r V`), the surjectivity of the
evaluation maps and the vanishing of `RвҒұПҖ_{T*}` are the deferred (gap-blocked)
content recorded for the prover stage. -/
theorem quot_uniformRegularity {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
    (n p : в„•) (ОҰ : Polynomial в„ҡ) :
    вҲғ m : в„•, вҲҖ (T : Scheme.{u}) (_ПҶT : T вҹ¶ S)
      (twistE twistF twistG : в„Ө вҶ’ (projectiveSpace n T).Modules)
      (r : в„Ө), (m : в„Ө) вүӨ r вҶ’
        (вҲғ dE : в„•, IsLocallyFreeOfRank dE
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward
            (projectiveSpace.structureMorphism n T)).obj (twistE r))) вҲ§
        (вҲғ dF : в„•, IsLocallyFreeOfRank dF
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward
            (projectiveSpace.structureMorphism n T)).obj (twistF r))) вҲ§
        (вҲғ dG : в„•, IsLocallyFreeOfRank dG
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward
            (projectiveSpace.structureMorphism n T)).obj (twistG r))) :=
  sorry

/-! ## Embedding Quot into the Grassmannian -/

/-- **Embedding of Quot into a Grassmannian** (`lem:quot-embed-grassmannian`).

For a fixed `r вүҘ m`, sending `вҹЁF, qвҹ©` to the rank-`ОҰ(r)` locally free quotient
`ПҖ_{T*}(q(r)) : ПҖ_{T*}E_T(r) вҶ’ ПҖ_{T*}F(r)` defines an injective morphism of functors
`Оұ : Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{E/X/S} вҶ’ Grass(W вҠ— Sym^r V, ОҰ(r))`, and the Grassmannian functor is
representable (`Functors.grassmannian_represents`).

STATING-GAP: the relative Grassmannian `Grass(W вҠ— Sym^r V, ОҰ(r))` of a bundle is a
Mathlib gap (`Functors.grassmannianScheme` is the absolute `Grass(r,d)` over `в„Ө`);
its representing scheme over `S` is abstracted as `Gr : Over S`, with `Оұ` a
natural transformation into `pointsFunctor Gr`.  "Injective morphism of functors"
вҶҰ `Mono Оұ`.  The recovery of `q` from `ПҖ_{T*}(q(r))` (powering the injectivity) is
the deferred content. -/
theorem quot_embedGrassmannian {S X : Scheme.{u}} (ПҖ : X вҹ¶ S) (E L : X.Modules)
    (ОҰ : Polynomial в„ҡ) (m : в„•) :
    вҲғ r : в„•, m вүӨ r вҲ§
      вҲғ (Gr : Over S) (Оұ : quotFunctorPhi ПҖ E L ОҰ вҹ¶ pointsFunctor Gr), Mono Оұ :=
  sorry

/-! ## Use of the flattening stratification -/

/-- **The representing flattening stratum** (`lem:quot-flattening-stratum`).

The morphism `Оұ` of `quot_embedGrassmannian` is relatively representable by locally
closed immersions: its image is the flattening stratum
(`FlatteningStratification.flatteningStratification`) for the universal quotient over
the Grassmannian at Hilbert polynomial `ОҰ`.  Hence `Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
represented by a locally closed subscheme, giving a locally closed embedding
`Q вҠӮ в„ҷ(вӢҖ^{ОҰ(r)}(W вҠ— Sym^r V))`; in particular `Q вҹ¶ S` is separated and of finite
type.

STATING-GAP: representability is `вү… pointsFunctor Q`; the projective bundle
`в„ҷ(вӢҖ^{ОҰ(r)}(W вҠ— Sym^r V))` (whose `вӢҖ`, `вҠ—`, `Sym^r` are Mathlib gaps) is shadowed
by `relativeProj S F` for an existentially-quantified `F`; "locally closed
embedding" вҶҰ `IsImmersion`.  Separatedness and finite type are stated directly. -/
theorem quot_flatteningStratum {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (ПҖ : X вҹ¶ S)
    (E L : X.Modules) (ОҰ : Polynomial в„ҡ) :
    вҲғ (Q : Over S) (F : S.Modules),
      Nonempty (quotFunctorPhi ПҖ E L ОҰ вү… pointsFunctor Q) вҲ§
      (вҲғ i : Q.left вҹ¶ relativeProj S F,
        IsImmersion i вҲ§ i вү« relativeProj.structureMorphism S F = Q.hom) вҲ§
      IsSeparated Q.hom вҲ§ LocallyOfFiniteType Q.hom :=
  sorry

/-! ## Valuative criterion for properness -/

/-- **Properness via the valuative criterion** (`lem:quot-properness`).

`Quot^{ОҰ,L}_{E/X/S}` satisfies the valuative criterion of properness over `S`.
Combined with finite type over the noetherian `S`, the representing scheme `Q вҹ¶ S`
is proper (via `Basic.valuativeCriterion_proper`, i.e.
`IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion`); hence the locally closed embedding of
`quot_flatteningStratum` into `в„ҷ(F)` is in fact a closed embedding.

STATING-GAP: the functor-level valuative criterion (bijectivity of
`Quot(Spec R) вҶ’ Quot(Spec K)` for DVRs `R`) is shadowed by the morphism-level
`ValuativeCriterion Q.hom` hypothesis on the representing scheme `Q`.  Properness is
discharged from `valuativeCriterion_proper`; "a proper locally closed immersion is a
closed immersion" is the remaining deferred geometric fact. -/
theorem quot_properness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (ПҖ : X вҹ¶ S)
    (E L : X.Modules) (ОҰ : Polynomial в„ҡ)
    (Q : Over S) [QuasiCompact Q.hom] [QuasiSeparated Q.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Q.hom]
    (_hrep : Nonempty (quotFunctorPhi ПҖ E L ОҰ вү… pointsFunctor Q))
    (hvc : ValuativeCriterion Q.hom) :
    IsProper Q.hom вҲ§
      вҲҖ (F : S.Modules) (i : Q.left вҹ¶ relativeProj S F),
        IsImmersion i вҶ’ i вү« relativeProj.structureMorphism S F = Q.hom вҶ’
        IsClosedImmersion i := by
  -- First conjunct: properness from the valuative criterion over the noetherian base.
  refine вҹЁvaluativeCriterion_proper Q.hom hvc, ?_вҹ©
  -- Second conjunct: a proper locally-closed immersion into `в„ҷ(F)` over `S` is a closed
  -- immersion.  The argument is the standard cancellation: `i вү« p = Q.hom` is proper and
  -- the structure morphism `p = relativeProj.structureMorphism S F` is separated, so `i`
  -- itself is proper (`IsProper.of_comp_of_isSeparated`); a proper morphism is universally
  -- closed, hence has closed range, and an immersion (= preimmersion) with closed range is a
  -- closed immersion (`IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion`).
  intro F i hi hcomp
  haveI hi' : IsImmersion i := hi
  haveI hQ : IsProper Q.hom := valuativeCriterion_proper Q.hom hvc
  -- GAP (relativeProj BUILD): separatedness of the relative-`Proj` structure morphism.
  -- `relativeProj` and its structure morphism are EGA-III stubs (`Basic.relativeProj` is
  -- `:= sorry`), so `IsSeparated (relativeProj.structureMorphism S F)` вҖ” a genuine theorem
  -- for the real relative `Proj`, which is separated over its base вҖ” is not yet derivable.
  -- This is the single named fact this proof is reduced to; everything else below is
  -- discharged from current Mathlib.  Tracked as a relativeProj-separatedness obligation.
  haveI hsep : IsSeparated (relativeProj.structureMorphism S F) := sorry
  -- `i вү« p = Q.hom` is proper, so by cancellation `i` is proper.
  haveI hcompProper : IsProper (i вү« relativeProj.structureMorphism S F) := by
    rw [hcomp]; infer_instance
  haveI hiProper : IsProper i :=
    IsProper.of_comp i (relativeProj.structureMorphism S F)
  -- Proper вҹ№ universally closed вҹ№ closed map вҹ№ closed range.
  haveI : UniversallyClosed i := hiProper.toUniversallyClosed
  have hrange : IsClosed (Set.range i.base.hom) := i.isClosedMap.isClosed_range
  -- Immersion вҹ№ preimmersion; preimmersion with closed range вҹ№ closed immersion.
  exact IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion i hrange

end MR2223407ConstructionHilbertQuot
